title: quercus morii
intro: Quercus morii, also known as the Korean corkbark oak, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive corky bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus morii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Quercus morii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
